evals.prompt: This OrderManager class in evals/files/eval-1-god-class/ is getting out of hand. The process_order method alone is like 150 lines and does validation, pricing, payment, shipping, and emails all in one giant function. The cancel_order and generate_report methods are also pretty bloated. Can you refactor this to be more maintainable? There are some basic tests in tests/ but coverage is pretty thin. The code works fine right now, I just need it cleaned up so it's easier to work with.
evals.expected_output: The OrderManager class is broken into smaller, focused classes or methods. process_order is decomposed into separate concerns (validation, pricing, payment, shipping, notification). Tests are expanded to cover the refactored code. All existing tests still pass. Complexity is measurably reduced.

assertions.text: The agent identified specific code smells (god class, long method, feature envy) before refactoring
process_order was decomposed into at least 3 smaller methods or classes with single responsibilities
No function in the refactored code has cyclomatic complexity greater than 10
Existing tests still pass after refactoring (behavior preserved)
New tests were added to cover the refactored code, achieving higher coverage than before
Refactoring was done incrementally (multiple small commits or steps, not one big rewrite)
External behavior of the class is preserved - same inputs produce same outputs

evals.prompt: I've got this report_service.py in evals/files/eval-2-ai-duplication/ that was mostly generated by AI and it's got a ton of copy-paste duplication. There are three nearly identical get_*_orders methods, three nearly identical get_*_revenue methods, three nearly identical format_*_report methods, and three customer query methods that all build the same customer dict. Can you consolidate all this duplication? There are a couple of basic tests in tests/ already.
evals.expected_output: Duplicated methods are consolidated into parameterized versions. get_daily/weekly/monthly_orders become a single get_orders(start, end) or similar. Revenue methods are consolidated similarly. Format methods share a common formatter. Customer query methods share aggregation logic. Tests are updated and all pass.

assertions.text: The agent identified the duplication patterns (orders, revenue, format, customer queries) before making changes
The 3 get_*_orders methods were consolidated into 1-2 parameterized methods
The 3 get_*_revenue methods were consolidated into 1-2 parameterized methods
The 3 format_*_report methods were consolidated or share common formatting logic
The 3 customer query methods (active/inactive/vip) share customer aggregation logic
Average cyclomatic complexity per method is reduced compared to the original code
Existing tests still pass after refactoring
New tests were added for the consolidated methods

evals.prompt: I need to add a new notification channel to this notification engine in evals/files/eval-3-tangled-untested/ but the send_notification method is really hard to follow - it's got deeply nested conditionals for quiet hours, rate limiting, template rendering, and then separate blocks for each channel (email, sms, push) all tangled together. Worse, there are basically no tests (the test file is empty). Before I add the new channel, can you refactor this to be more maintainable and add proper test coverage? Don't add the new channel yet, just clean up what's there.
evals.expected_output: Characterization tests are written first to capture current behavior. Then the tangled send_notification method is refactored into clearer concerns (quiet hours check, rate limiting, template rendering, channel dispatch). Each channel handler is extracted. Tests cover the refactored code at 90%+. All behavior is preserved.

assertions.text: Characterization tests were written BEFORE any refactoring changes were made
The quiet hours logic was extracted into a separate method or function
The rate limiting logic was extracted into a separate method or function
Individual channel handlers (email, sms, push) were extracted into separate methods
The refactored send_notification method is shorter and reads as a high-level workflow
Test coverage reaches at least 80% on the refactored code
No new notification channel was added (the agent respected the scope boundary)
Refactoring was done incrementally with tests passing after each step

evals.prompt: We're getting runaway Lambda invocations and huge AWS bills on this order processing pipeline in evals/files/eval-4-lambda-loop/. It's a Step Function that orchestrates 4 Lambdas (validator, enricher, payment, restock) with an SNS topic and SQS queues connecting them. I think there are circular event paths causing infinite loops but I can't figure out exactly where. The CDK stack is in stacks/pipeline_stack.py and the Lambda handlers are in src/. Can you map out the event flows, identify all the loops, and refactor the architecture to break them? There are a couple of basic tests but they don't test the event flow at all.
evals.expected_output: All circular event paths are identified and documented with clear diagrams or descriptions. The architecture is refactored to break the loops — e.g., SNS subscription filters added, DynamoDB stream trigger removed or guarded, payment failure path fixed to not re-trigger validator, enricher idempotency skip fixed. The Step Function definition is cleaned up. Tests added for the event flow logic.

assertions.text: The agent identified at least 3 distinct circular event paths before making changes
Loop 1 identified: enricher publishes to SNS -> enricher queue (no filter) -> enricher re-triggers itself
Loop 2 identified: enricher -> restock queue -> restock handler -> inventory update -> DynamoDB stream -> enricher
Loop 3 identified: payment failure -> SNS -> validator re-processes order -> Step Function restarts
Loop 4 identified: Step Function invokes enricher directly AND enricher also triggered via SNS (double processing)
SNS subscription filters were added or separate topics created to prevent unintended cross-triggering
The enricher's fall-through bug (publishing enrichment_complete even when skipping) was fixed
The DynamoDB stream -> enricher loop was broken (stream removed, or enricher guards against stream-triggered re-enrichment)
The Step Function re-enrich -> payment loop has a bounded retry limit or circuit breaker
Tests were added that verify the event flow doesn't create circular invocations
